meta.source_paper: Weintraub et al. 2022 (blockchain RPC privacy); EF Privacy & Scaling Explorations research

risk_domain.name: RPC Query Pattern Leakage
risk_domain.description: Privacy leakage through RPC query patterns that reveal user intent, address ownership, portfolio composition, and trading behavior to RPC providers and network observers

capabilities: full observation of all RPC queries from user to provider (RPC provider or network eavesdropper)
timing correlation across queries within a session
cross-session user identification via IP, API key, or query fingerprint
correlation of query patterns with on-chain state changes
aggregation of query metadata across multiple users for pattern analysis
limitations: cannot observe queries routed through Tor or encrypted tunnels without endpoint compromise
cannot distinguish cover queries from real queries if properly constructed
cannot access local execution results (Helios light client)

H1_balance_check_linkage.name: Balance checks linking addresses
H1_balance_check_linkage.description: User queries eth_getBalance for multiple addresses within a short time window from the same session, revealing that these addresses are controlled by or of interest to the same entity. This is the simplest and most common address-linkage leak at the RPC layer.

signals.name: multi_address_balance_burst
signals.description: eth_getBalance called for 2+ distinct addresses within a 60-second window from the same session

signals.name: periodic_balance_sweep
signals.description: Same set of addresses queried repeatedly on a schedule (e.g., every 5 minutes), indicating portfolio monitoring

signals.name: balance_after_transfer
signals.description: eth_getBalance query for an address immediately after that address receives a transfer, correlating the query origin with the transfer recipient

detection.threshold: multi_address_balance_burst with 3+ addresses triggers BLOCK; 2 addresses triggers WARNING

recommendations.description: Pad balance queries with cover addresses (random valid addresses) to obscure which addresses the user actually controls
Run a local light client (Helios) to query balances without revealing query patterns to a remote provider
recommendations.user_cost: bandwidth and compute for light client sync

recommendations.description: Route each balance query through a different Tor circuit to prevent session linkage

H1_balance_check_linkage.fundamental_limitation: Any remote RPC provider can observe query patterns. Even with cover queries, a provider seeing queries from the same IP/session can apply statistical analysis over time to separate real from cover queries. True privacy requires local execution.

benchmark_scenario.setup: User with N addresses queries balances through a monitored RPC endpoint. Adversary attempts to cluster addresses by session.
benchmark_scenario.metric: Fraction of user's address set correctly identified by the adversary
benchmark_scenario.baseline: Without mitigation, 100% linkable for same-session queries

H2_position_monitoring.name: Position monitoring via repeated eth_call
H2_position_monitoring.description: Repeated eth_call to DeFi protocol read methods (getPosition, balanceOf, getReserves) reveals the user's DeFi positions, LP holdings, and lending health factors. Patterns of position monitoring reveal portfolio composition even without direct balance queries.

signals.name: repeated_position_read
signals.description: eth_call to the same DeFi contract read method (e.g., balanceOf, getPosition) repeated more than 5 times in an hour for the same target address

signals.name: multi_protocol_scan
signals.description: eth_call queries fan out across multiple DeFi protocols (Aave, Compound, Uniswap, Maker) for the same address within a session

signals.name: health_factor_monitoring
signals.description: Repeated queries to lending protocol health factor or liquidation price methods, indicating leveraged position monitoring

detection.threshold: multi_protocol_scan triggers WARNING; repeated_position_read with >10 queries/hour triggers BLOCK

recommendations.description: Aggregate all position queries into a single multicall and pad with queries for positions the user does not hold
Use a local light client to sync relevant state and compute positions locally without per-query RPC calls

H2_position_monitoring.fundamental_limitation: DeFi position monitoring inherently requires reading on-chain state. Reducing query frequency sacrifices real-time awareness (critical for liquidation protection). The tradeoff is privacy vs position safety.

benchmark_scenario.setup: Simulated user with positions across N DeFi protocols. Adversary observes RPC queries and attempts to reconstruct the user's portfolio.
benchmark_scenario.metric: Fraction of user's DeFi positions correctly identified by the adversary from query patterns alone
benchmark_scenario.baseline: Without mitigation, all monitored positions are trivially identifiable

H3_pretrade_intent.name: Pre-trade intent via swap simulation
H3_pretrade_intent.description: User simulates swap transactions via eth_call before submitting them, revealing trading intent (which token, direction, size) to the RPC provider. This enables front-running, sandwich attacks, or selling intent data to MEV searchers.

signals.name: swap_simulation
signals.description: eth_call targeting a DEX router contract (Uniswap, Sushiswap, 1inch) with swap function selectors

signals.name: simulation_then_submit
signals.description: eth_call swap simulation followed by eth_sendRawTransaction for the same swap within 60 seconds

signals.name: multiple_route_simulations
signals.description: Multiple eth_call simulations to different DEX contracts for the same token pair, indicating price comparison before trade

detection.threshold: simulation_then_submit triggers BLOCK; swap_simulation alone triggers WARNING

recommendations.description: Run swap simulations against local state (Helios light client or cached state) instead of remote eth_call
recommendations.user_cost: requires local node or state cache

recommendations.description: Route swap simulations through a private/encrypted RPC endpoint (Flashbots Protect, MEV Blocker) that commits to not selling intent data
Pad real swap simulation with fake simulations for other token pairs to obscure actual trading intent

H3_pretrade_intent.fundamental_limitation: Local simulation requires fresh on-chain state, which itself may require RPC queries to fetch. Fully private simulation requires either a full local node or trusted execution environment. Private RPCs are trust-based (no cryptographic guarantee they won't leak).

benchmark_scenario.setup: User simulates N swap transactions through a monitored RPC. Adversary attempts to extract trading intent and front-run.
benchmark_scenario.metric: Fraction of swap intents correctly identified by the adversary before on-chain submission
benchmark_scenario.baseline: Without mitigation, 100% of simulated swaps reveal full intent to the RPC provider

H4_stealth_address_scanning.name: Stealth address scanning pattern
H4_stealth_address_scanning.description: User scans for incoming stealth address payments by querying the ERC-5564 announcement contract logs. The scanning pattern (frequency, filter parameters, view key derivation queries) reveals the user as a stealth address recipient and may leak the stealth meta-address.

signals.name: announcement_log_scan
signals.description: eth_getLogs queries targeting the ERC-5564 Announcer contract with specific topic filters matching stealth address announcement events

signals.name: periodic_scan_pattern
signals.description: Repeated eth_getLogs to the announcement contract at regular intervals, indicating automated stealth address scanning

signals.name: narrow_block_range
signals.description: eth_getLogs queries with narrow block ranges (scanning incrementally for new announcements), revealing an active stealth address user

detection.threshold: periodic_scan_pattern with >3 scans/hour triggers WARNING; announcement_log_scan from non-anonymized session triggers WARNING

recommendations.description: Run stealth address scanning against a local light client to avoid revealing scan patterns to remote RPC providers
recommendations.user_cost: bandwidth and compute for light client

recommendations.description: Route stealth address scan queries through Tor with rotating circuits to prevent session linkage
recommendations.user_cost: high latency, may miss time-sensitive payments

recommendations.description: Pad stealth address scans with cover queries for other contract logs to obscure the scanning pattern

H4_stealth_address_scanning.fundamental_limitation: Stealth address recipients must scan the announcement contract to discover payments. This is inherent to the protocol design (ERC-5564). Without a trusted server or TEE-based scanning service, the scanning pattern always leaks to whoever serves the query.

benchmark_scenario.setup: N stealth address users scanning via a shared RPC endpoint. Adversary observes query patterns and attempts to identify stealth address recipients.
benchmark_scenario.metric: Fraction of stealth address users correctly identified from their scanning patterns
benchmark_scenario.baseline: Regular periodic scans to the announcement contract are trivially identifiable

H5_price_check_correlation.name: Token price checking correlated with holdings
H5_price_check_correlation.description: User queries token prices or DEX pool reserves for specific tokens, and this pattern correlates with their actual holdings. Repeated price monitoring for a narrow set of tokens reveals portfolio composition even without direct balance queries.

signals.name: selective_price_monitoring
signals.description: eth_call to DEX pool contracts (getReserves, slot0) for a consistent small set of token pairs across sessions

signals.name: price_then_trade
signals.description: Price check query for a token pair followed by a swap transaction for the same pair within 5 minutes

signals.name: holding_correlated_frequency
signals.description: Price check frequency for a token correlates with the user's on-chain balance of that token (more frequent checks for larger positions)

detection.threshold: price_then_trade triggers WARNING; selective_price_monitoring over 3+ sessions triggers WARNING

recommendations.description: Query prices for a broad set of tokens (top 50 by market cap) regardless of holdings, to obscure which tokens the user actually cares about
Fetch token prices from public aggregator APIs (CoinGecko, DeFi Llama) over Tor instead of querying on-chain pools via RPC
recommendations.user_cost: prices may be slightly delayed vs on-chain

H5_price_check_correlation.fundamental_limitation: Price checking is correlated with trading intent by nature. Cover queries add noise but a persistent adversary can filter them out over many sessions using consistency analysis. Truly private price feeds require local execution or oblivious query protocols that don't yet exist at scale.

benchmark_scenario.setup: User with known portfolio monitors prices via RPC. Adversary observes query patterns over N sessions and attempts to infer holdings.
benchmark_scenario.metric: Adversary accuracy in identifying top-5 token holdings from price query patterns alone
benchmark_scenario.baseline: Without cover queries, selective price monitoring reveals holdings with high accuracy

helios_local.description: Run Ethereum light client locally to execute eth_call, eth_getBalance, and eth_getLogs against verified state without revealing query patterns to remote RPC providers

tor_routing.description: Route RPC queries through Tor with per-query circuit rotation to prevent session linkage and IP-based user identification

query_batching.description: Aggregate multiple RPC queries into a single multicall to reduce the number of observable query events and add cover queries in the same batch

cover_queries.description: Generate plausible cover queries (random valid addresses, popular token pairs, trending contract calls) to pad real queries and obscure user intent

combined_benchmark.description: End-to-end benchmark measuring RPC provider's ability to infer user identity, portfolio, and trading intent from query patterns

methodology.step_1: Simulate N users with known portfolios and trading patterns generating RPC queries through a monitored endpoint
methodology.step_2: Run adversary simulator applying all 5 heuristics to the query log
methodology.step_3: Measure baseline inference accuracy (address linkage, portfolio reconstruction, intent detection)
methodology.step_4: Apply profile recommendations (cover queries, local execution, Tor routing) to all users
methodology.step_5: Re-run adversary simulator on modified query patterns
methodology.step_6: Compare: inference accuracy before vs after mitigation

combined_benchmark.target: Reduce adversary inference accuracy from >90% to <20% for address linkage, <30% for portfolio reconstruction

adversary_levels.name: basic
adversary_levels.description: Balance checks and price correlation only

adversary_levels.name: intermediate
adversary_levels.description: Add position monitoring and pre-trade intent

adversary_levels.name: advanced
adversary_levels.description: All heuristics including stealth address scanning

templates.risk_assessment: RPC query pattern triggers {heuristic_id} ({heuristic_name}): {signal_description}. Confidence: {confidence}. Recommendation: {recommendation}.
templates.summary: Analyzed {n_queries} RPC queries in session. {n_risky} patterns flagged ({pct_risky}%). Leaked information: {leaked_info}. Top risks: {top_risks}.
